FULL CONTENT:
![](https://ik.imagekit.io/croplife/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/GAA_BRA_F6B0078-700x467.jpg?v=1694706417)

# Rational Regulation for Plant Breeding Innovations

CropLife International recognizes and champions the beneficial societal impacts of plant breeding innovations, most recently and specifically the application of genome (or gene) editing technology. Genome editing can contribute toward efficient and sustainable agricultural practices by accelerating traditional plant breeding practices and improving disease resistance, climate resilience, nutritional quality, and yield. Scientists and breeders have already demonstrated the range of possibilities; however, utilization of these technologies across a broad segment of crops and countries will depend on the regulatory policies applied upon them.

CropLife International and the International Seed Federation (ISF) share the strong belief that: **“Plant varieties developed through the latest plant breeding methods should not be differentially regulated if they are similar or indistinguishable from varieties that could have been produced through earlier plant breeding methods.”** Read the full position of ISF [here](https://worldseed.org/wp-content/uploads/2018/06/Plant-breeding-innovation-Consistent-criteria-for-the-scope-of-regulatory-oversight.pdf).

As of 2023, more than 30 regulatory bodies have released guidance, policies, and regulations, many of which are highly aligned with the principles outlined above and provide a regulatory pathway for products utilizing genome editing that is aligned with conventional varieties. CropLife International also supports dialogue and information sharing between the seed sector, government, grain trade, and other value chain members including food processors, retailers, and consumer groups to ensure this technology delivers maximum benefit to farmers and society.

CropLife International and ISF have developed a number of resources to support further technical discussion of policy around plant breeding innovations, including an [FAQ on Plant Breeding Innovations.](https://worldseed.org/resources/faqs/)

## Related Resources

### Potential Impact of Genome Editing on Climate Adaptation and Mitigation

CropLife International’s report on genome editing highlights how gene editing technologies, such as CRISPR, can bolster climate adaptation and mitigation in agriculture. The report explores how targeted genetic improvements can enhance crop resilience to extreme weather, increase carbon sequestration, and improve crop yields while reducing environmental impact.

### Technical Summaries of New Plant Breeding Techniques (NBTs)

"CropLife International's technical summary on New Plant Breeding Techniques (NBTs) provides insights into innovative methods like CRISPR, cisgenesis, RNA-dependent DNA methylation, and agroinfiltration. The document outlines the precision and efficiency of these tools in enhancing crop traits while addressing regulatory considerations and their alignment with sustainable agriculture goals."

### Information Sharing & Transparency in Plant Breeding

CropLife International (CLI) emphasizes transparency and responsible practices in plant breeding, advocating for open information sharing by member companies on seed varieties. CLI supports voluntary food labeling to help consumers make informed choices, while stressing that traceability agreements among breeders, suppliers, and consumers can best facilitate product differentiation. In addition, CLI promotes responsible stewardship for plant breeding innovations, including genome editing, and supports third-party verification programs to enhance quality and safety. This position underscores CLI’s commitment to effective communication, traceability, and proactive engagement across the agricultural value chain to build consumer trust.

enriched_points.point_title: Need for safety in agricultural innovation
enriched_points.expanded_content: Regulations ensure that new breeding techniques (NBTs) prioritize food safety and environmental protection, addressing potential risks associated with genetic modifications.

enriched_points.point_title: Impact of regulations on NBT adoption
enriched_points.expanded_content: Stringent regulations can hinder the swift adoption of NBTs, limiting their integration into agricultural practices and preventing advancements in crop resilience and yield.
Effective regulations must consider public perceptions of biotechnology, ensuring transparency and trust while fostering innovative solutions to global food security challenges.
